tool_args.label: Bridges and Tunnels Key Performance Metrics April 2026
tool_args.raw_text: This performance metrics document was prepared for the April 2026 meeting of the Bridges and Tunnels Committee.

payload.title: Bridges and Tunnels Key Performance Metrics
payload.description: Key performance metrics document for the April 2026 meeting of the MTA Bridges and Tunnels Committee covering traffic, safety, enforcement, financial results, and major projects.

tool_args.label: MTA Bridges and Tunnels
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Bridges and Tunnels is comprised of over 1,000 employees and operates seven bridges and two tunnels in New York City, handling over 330 million vehicle crossings every year in addition to managing the Congestion Relief Zone.

payload.description: Operates seven bridges and two tunnels in New York City, handling over 330 million vehicle crossings every year in addition to managing the Congestion Relief Zone.

tool_args.raw_text: Catherine Sheridan President, Bridges and Tunnels

tool_args.label: Marine Parkway Bridge Gantry Lane Use Signals
tool_args.raw_text: A project to improve safety and security at the Marine Parkway Bridge, led jointly by MTA Construction & Development and MTA Bridges and Tunnels Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) Division, is in its early stages. The $4.2 million contract, which involves replacement of traffic lane control signals and upgrades to security cameras, was awarded in December 2025 and is scheduled for completion in March of 2027.

payload.project_name: Marine Parkway Bridge Gantry Lane Use Signals
payload.description: Improve safety and security at the Marine Parkway Bridge. Replacement of traffic lane control signals and upgrades to security cameras. New fiber-optic, programmable logic controller-based system for lane-use and bridge lift traffic control signals. New CCTV security cameras.
payload.partner_agency: MTA Bridges and Tunnels Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) Division

evidence_refs.source_quote: A project to improve safety and security at the Marine Parkway Bridge... The $4.2 million contract... was awarded in December 2025 and is scheduled for completion in March of 2027.

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

tool_args.label: Total Traffic Change March 2026 vs March 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Traffic at MTA Bridges and Tunnels in March 2026 was lower by a total of 110,594 vehicles or 0.4%, compared to March 2025.

tool_args.label: Customer Collision Rate March 2026
tool_args.raw_text: In March 2026, MTA Bridges and Tunnels recorded a total collision rate of 2.3 per million vehicles, a 18.6% decrease from the rate of 2.7 per million vehicles recorded in March 2025.

payload.raw_value_text: total collision rate of 2.3 per million vehicles

tool_args.label: Collision Rate Percent Change March 2026
tool_args.raw_text: a 18.6% decrease from the rate of 2.7 per million vehicles recorded in March 2025

tool_args.label: Collisions-With-Injury Rate March 2026
tool_args.raw_text: The collisions-with-injury per million vehicles also declined to 0.5, a 32.1% reduction from last year's rate of 0.8.

tool_args.label: Lost-Time Injury Rate February 2026
tool_args.raw_text: The lost-time injury rate for February 2026 was 3.2, which constitutes a 30.6% decrease relative to February 2025, when the rate was 4.6.

tool_args.label: National Work Zone Awareness Week
tool_args.raw_text: As construction season begins, we recently stood with transportation agencies statewide in recognizing National Work Zone Awareness Week at events in Albany and Ardsley, which highlighted the continued rollout of Automated Work Zone Speed Enforcement and this year's theme, 'Safe Actions Save Lives.'

payload.claim_text: MTA Bridges and Tunnels participated in National Work Zone Awareness Week at events in Albany and Ardsley, highlighting the continued rollout of Automated Work Zone Speed Enforcement with theme 'Safe Actions Save Lives.'
payload.description: MTA Bridges and Tunnels recognized National Work Zone Awareness Week with statewide transportation agencies
payload.subject: MTA Bridges and Tunnels

tool_args.label: E-ZPass Customer Service Center Pilot
tool_args.raw_text: the debut of our E-ZPass Customer Service Center pilot. Our on-site presence resulted in improved customer understanding of E-ZPass, helped address concerns, and enabled more meaningful interactions with more than 1,000 customers.

payload.claim_text: MTA Bridges and Tunnels debuted an E-ZPass Customer Service Center pilot at the New York International Auto Show, resulting in improved customer understanding and more than 1,000 customer interactions.
payload.description: E-ZPass Customer Service Center pilot debuted at the New York International Auto Show
